theorem_statement: Let P be a Euclidean witness plane with unit normal n_P, and let T be a rigid oriented triangular face initially incident with P. Assume T may rotate about its centroid into one chosen half-space without folding through P, while preserving its intrinsic edge lengths, orientation, and chamber receipt. Let gamma be an incoming directed path intercepted by T. If an admissible transfer carried by T sends gamma into an output direction parallel to n_P, then the receipt on P is orthogonal to the witness plane, independent of the triangle's in-face heading. The page receives the delivered output trace, while the lifted triangular face remains part of the hidden transport provenance rather than the page-visible receipt.

objects.receipt: R_P = projection of O_T(gamma) onto P

boundary: Finite Euclidean witness-transfer theorem under explicit hypotheses.
The triangle is a rigid oriented face, not a physical optical device.
The page normal is a Euclidean metric direction.
Nonpenetration and one-sided lifting are imposed boundary conditions.
Orthogonal output delivery is a theorem hypothesis, not an optical law derived here.
The page records the delivered receipt, not the full hidden transfer provenance.
No reflection, refraction, prism, CMB-geodesic, retinal, Higgs, physics, force, or Gap A closure claim.
